M-058.NAME: mtproto-admin-analytics-ui
M-058.owns: Compact admin UI for MTProto usage analytics, per-proxy details, abuse signals, runtime health, availability proof, and promotion tag controls.
M-058.purpose: Let the operator analyze MTProto usage from desktop or mobile admin panel without exposing secrets.

exports.view-mtproto-analytics: Admin page or MTProto tab for global metrics, traffic windows, active connections, top users, and error summaries.
exports.view-mtproto-assignment-detail: Per-proxy detail panel with last seen, session count, duration, traffic, errors, and abuse flags.
exports.view-mtproto-user-investigation: Phase-43 compact user/proxy drawer with issued time, last seen, current IP, last IP, 90-day IP history, sessions, graphs, and errors.
exports.view-mtproto-alert-inbox: Phase-43 admin alert inbox for high/critical abuse signals with acknowledge, resolve, disable proxy, and TTL IP block actions.
exports.view-mtproto-promotion-tag: Promotion tag form showing masked current tag, validation state, apply status, and pending restart state.
exports.smoke-admin-analytics-redaction: Static smoke ensuring UI bundles do not render full tg links, secrets, tokens, or raw tags.

M-058.evidence: pass-local 2026-05-21: frontend-admin build and Phase-42/Phase-43 admin analytics static smokes passed; UI removes Recent events, paginates large proxy/user lists, exposes compact area graphs, clarifies source-IP unavailable states, splits Abuse into open inbox plus archive, and standardizes icon input padding.

ui-policy.policy-1: Mobile layout uses dense summary rows, tabs, filters, and drill-down panels rather than large decorative dashboards.
ui-policy.policy-2: Promotion tag editing requires confirmation and never displays the full stored tag after save.
ui-policy.policy-3: Abuse signals are visibly observe-only in Phase-42.
ui-policy.policy-4: Phase-43 removes the visible Recent events panel and replaces it with compact Overview, Users, Abuse, and Settings surfaces.
ui-policy.policy-5: Raw IP values may render only inside the explicit admin investigation drawer and must never appear in general analytics cards or static smoke output.
ui-policy.policy-6: Large assignment/user inventories must use compact paging and bounded scroll panes so the admin panel remains usable with thousands of proxies.
ui-policy.policy-7: Abuse actions remove items from the open inbox by querying only open alerts, while acknowledged/resolved alerts remain visible in an archive list.

constraints.constraint-1: Admin UI must not hide existing MTProto reissue/revoke controls.
constraints.constraint-2: Frontend build and mobile smoke must pass before phase closure.

M-058.hotspot: H-045: Admin analytics UI must remain compact on mobile while exposing enough filters for incident triage
